source_description: In an apartment living room, Joey, Chandler, and Monica sit on a sofa facing forward, while Phoebe stands behind them. An off-screen voice says, "Now you've seen everybody. And better yet, they've all seen you."

In an apartment living room, Joey, Chandler, and Monica sit on a sofa facing forward, while Phoebe stands behind them. An off-screen voice says, "Now you've seen everybody. And better yet, they've all seen you."
A woman, Rachel, opens a door and enters the room wearing a dark shirt and shorts, rubbing her eye. She closes the door behind her. Monica turns her head toward Rachel.
A woman, Rachel, opens a door and enters the room wearing a dark shirt and shorts, rubbing her eye. She closes the door behind her. Monica turns her head toward Rachel.
A woman, Rachel, opens a door and enters the room wearing a dark shirt and shorts, rubbing her eye. She closes the door behind her. Monica turns her head toward Rachel.
Monica gestures with one hand and asks, "Rachel, how did this happen?" Rachel steps forward, gesturing with both hands, and replies, "I don't know. I just kind of ran into him last night."
Monica gestures with one hand and asks, "Rachel, how did this happen?" Rachel steps forward, gesturing with both hands, and replies, "I don't know. I just kind of ran into him last night."
Phoebe, standing behind the sofa, asks, "Where?" Rachel points her thumb back and says, "At his apartment." She then kneels by a nearby table, points to a pitcher, and asks, "Is this juice?"
Phoebe, standing behind the sofa, asks, "Where?" Rachel points her thumb back and says, "At his apartment." She then kneels by a nearby table, points to a pitcher, and asks, "Is this juice?"
Rachel begins pouring a glass of juice from the pitcher.
Joey and Chandler lean forward and simultaneously say, "Whoa, whoa." Chandler reaches toward his neck. Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler gestures toward Rachel with an open hand and asks, "And the fact that you dumped him because he hit on Phoebe?" Rachel holds her filled juice glass.
Chandler gestures toward Rachel with an open hand and asks, "And the fact that you dumped him because he hit on Phoebe?" Rachel holds her filled juice glass.
Rachel sets the glass down, covers her face with both hands, and says, "Oh, God, I know. I'm pathetic. I'm a loser," prompting audience laughter.
Monica sits forward slightly, clasping her hands on her lap.
Monica unclasps her hands, gestures, and says, "Honey, you're not pathetic. You're sad." Chandler and Joey remain seated on the sofa.

Monica glances slightly downward, smiles, and finishes, "nickel for every guy I wish I hadn't" as she trails off. Audience laughter is heard.

Monica points both index fingers forward and says, "but this is about your horrible mistake."
